Q8 MCQ Not checked

Not attempted

In the following question, a part of the sentence is given in bold. Choose the correct alternative that best improves the bold part. If no improvement is needed, select “No improvement.”
The Vice Chancellor congratulated the blind student for his success in the examination.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The verb “congratulate” is typically followed by the preposition “on” when referring to the reason for the congratulation.
Correct sentence: “The Vice Chancellor congratulated the blind student on his success in the examination.”

Q9 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Read the passage carefully and choose the best answer to the following question from the four alternatives given below.
In the past 50 years, doctors across the world have accepted the practice to prescribe antibiotics at the first sign of a trivial infection or treat patients with a handful of antibiotics. These days it is not uncommon to see practitioners prescribing multiple antibiotics without any real indication or relevance for such a combination of drugs. Antibiotics have traditionally been known as miracle drugs, but there is growing evidence that they are overworked miracles, especially in countries like ours where there is easy access to drugs across the counter, including antibiotics. We cannot think of a return to pre-antibiotic days. Yet the unbridled use of these agents is inexorably propelling us in that direction.
Antibiotics are called overworked miracles because:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The passage clearly points out that antibiotics are being excessively used, so they are called overworked miracles.

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Average linkage clustering defines the distance between two clusters as the mean of all pairwise distances.
S2 : Since it uses average distances, average linkage is less influenced by outliers compared to single or complete linkage.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• In average linkage clustering, the distance between two clusters is computed as the average
of all pairwise distances between points in the two clusters. So, S1 is correct.
• Because it takes an average rather than just minimum or maximum, it is less affected by
extreme values or outliers compared to single linkage (chaining effect) and complete linkage
(sensitive to farthest points). So, S2 is correct.

Q31 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• Sigmoid activation compresses inputs into (0, 1), making gradients very small for large values
→ vanishing gradient problem. ReLU avoids this. So, option (a) is correct.
• Dying ReLU means some neurons output zero permanently, not all. The statement exaggerates,
so it is false. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• In single linkage clustering, merging is based on minimum distance → leads to the chaining
effect. So, option (c) is correct.
• ReLU does not saturate like sigmoid/tanh. For negative inputs, it just outputs 0 by design,
not due to saturation. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q32 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Let p, q and r be propositions and the expression:
(p ∨ q ∨ r) ∧ (¬p ∨ ¬q ∨ ¬r) is true for given conditions. Then which of the following is/are correct for given conditions.

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
(p ∨ q ∨ r) ∧ (¬p ∨ ¬q ∨ ¬r) is true when at least one of p, q and r is true and atleast one is false, but
is false when all three variables have the same truth value.

Q40 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Suppose there are n elements in the range [1 + n2]. Then counting sort performs better than selection sort in the worst case.
S2 : Selection of pivot element decides the time complexity of quick sort.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
As for the given range of elements, counting sort time complexity will be same as selection sort time complexity in worst case.
So, S1 is incorrect.

Q41 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Gibbs Sampling can suffer from slow mixing when the variables in the distribution are highly correlated.
S2 : Gibbs Sampling produces independent samples at each iteration without any correlation.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• When variables are highly correlated, Gibbs updates move slowly in the sample space, leading
to slow mixing and higher auto correlation. So, S1 is correct.
• Gibbs Sampling generates a Markov chain, and consecutive samples are correlated, not independent. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q43 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

In the context of using LDA for classification when the number of features exceeds the number of samples, cross-validation is mainly used to:

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
• Cross-validation allows us to repeatedly train/test on different splits to estimate error.
So, option (a) is correct.
• Incorrect. Dimensionality reduction methods like PCA reduce features, not cross-validation.
So, option (b) is incorrect.
• LDA does not guarantee a perfect separating hyperplane. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Cross-validation validates how well the model generalizes and helps tune parameters.
So, option (d) is correct.

Q61 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
• OLAP databases are structured in star/snowflake schemas because they support
multidimensional queries and provide efficient query performance for analysis. So, option (a)
is correct.
• OLTP (Online Transaction Processing) handles day-to-day operations (banking transactions,
reservations, retail purchases) with ACID properties ensuring correctness. So, option (b) is
correct.
• OLAP is designed for historical, aggregated, read-intensive data analysis. It is not real-time
or write-intensive. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• A star schema only contains fact tables (numerical data) and dimension tables (descriptive
attributes). It does not contain “all types” of data. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q64 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
• The Chi-square distribution is positively skewed, especially for small degrees of freedom. It becomes approximately symmetric only when degrees of freedom are large, but never perfectly symmetric. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• For large degrees of freedom, the Chi-square distribution tends toward a Normal distribution with mean df and variance 2 ⋅ df. So, option (b) is correct.
• That is the role of the t-test, not Chi-square. The Chi-square test deals with variances and categorical frequency data (goodness-of-fit, independence, homogeneity). So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Because it is a sum of squares of standard normal variables, the Chi-square distribution cannot take negative values. So, option (d) is correct.
